name: Institute for Understanding Anti-Palestinian Racism
theoryOfChange: The 'About' page on the Institute for the Understanding of Anti-Palestinian Racism (IUAPR) website describes 'The APR Project.' This project outlines the organization's strategic approach to addressing anti-Palestinian racism, including aims such as broadening conversations, bringing about institutional change, creating educational resources, and empowering community engagement. These aims function as a program strategy, detailing how their activities lead to their goals of combating anti-Palestinian racism.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2024 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: UNCORROBORATED: UCSF Institutional Review Board (IRB) approval for research

strengths.detail: The organization distinguishes itself by focusing on <cite id="1">data-driven advocacy and empirical study</cite> to identify systemic prejudice.
The Institute maintains a <cite id="7">clear theory of change</cite> and structured program areas including <cite id="4">education and strategic advocacy</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Institute's primary strength lies in its academic and institutional influence. By producing specialized reports such as <cite id="3">'Anti-Palestinian Racism: Naming, Framing and Manifestations'</cite>, the organization provides a vocabulary for addressing prejudice. Their research has been utilized in academic circles, contributing to a landscape where <cite id="5">40 faculty associations</cite> have engaged with their framing of institutional racism and academic freedom.
rich_narrative.summary: The Institute for the Understanding of Anti-Palestinian Racism Inc (IUAPR) is a specialized research and advocacy organization that addresses systemic prejudice through <cite id="1">empirical study and education</cite>. Operating as a <cite id="2">501(c)(3) non-profit organization</cite>, the Institute focuses on <cite id="3">naming and framing the manifestations</cite> of anti-Palestinian racism within institutional contexts. Its work is primarily structured around three core pillars: <cite id="4">empirical research, education and training, and strategic advocacy</cite>. A significant portion of its documented impact includes academic and institutional engagement, such as tracking <cite id="5">40 faculty associations that have rejected the IHRA definition</cite> of antisemitism in favor of frameworks that protect Palestinian speech. As an emerging organization, IUAPR provides a platform for <cite id="6">charitable giving through its digital presence</cite> to support the documentation of anti-Palestinian sentiment. While the organization maintains a <cite id="7">clear theory of change</cite> focused on capacity building, it currently operates with a <cite id="8">minimal board of two members</cite>, reflecting its early-stage maturity. The Institute serves a niche role in the advocacy landscape by providing <cite id="9">structured education and training programs</cite> designed to address systemic racism through data-driven insights rather than traditional humanitarian service delivery.
